title: Renouf Atoni
wikitext:
{{short description|New Zealand rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2022}} {{Use New Zealand English|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox rugby league biography | name = Renouf Atoni | fullname = Renouf To'omaga-Atoni | image = Renouf To'omaga.jpg | image_size = 285 | position = {{rlp|LF|PR}} | birth_date = Renouf To'omaga
{{Birth date and age|1995|6|25|df=y}} | birth_place = [[Porirua]], New Zealand | height = {{convert|1.90|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} | weight = {{convert|110|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} | club1 = {{nowrap|[[Canterbury Bulldogs]]}} | year1start = 2018 | year1end = 21 | appearances1 = 43 | tries1 = 3 | goals1 = 0 | fieldgoals1 = 0 | points1 = 12 | club2 = {{nowrap|[[Sydney Roosters]]}} | year2start = 2022 | year2end = | appearances2 = 0 | tries2 = 0 | goals2 = 0 | fieldgoals2 = 0 | points2 = 0 | club3 = {{nowrap|[[Wakefield Trinity]]}} | year3start = 2023 | year3end = 25 | appearances3 = 66 | tries3 = 12 | goals3 = 0 | fieldgoals3 = 0 | points3 = 48 | club4 = {{nowrap|[[Castleford Tigers]]}} | year4start = 2026 | year4end = present | appearances4 = 12 | tries4 = 0 | goals4 = 0 | fieldgoals4 = 0 | points4 = 0 | new = yes | source = [https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/renouf-to--omaga/summary.html Rugby League Project][https://www.bulldogs.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/canterbury-bankstown-bulldogs/renouf-atoni/ NRL] | updated = 30 April 2026 }} '''Renouf Atoni''' (''[[Given name#Name at birth|né]]'' '''To'omaga'''; born 25 June 1995) is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). ==Background== Atoni was born in [[Porirua]], New Zealand. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] (through Mother) and [[Tokelau]]an (through Father) descent. His junior club was the [[Porirua Vikings]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2021/01/20/from-toomaga-to-atoni-the-story-behind-renoufs-name-change/ |title=From To'omaga to Atoni: The story behind Renouf's name change |publisher=Bulldogs.com.au |date=19 January 2021}} ==Playing career== 2014: Renouf received the Holden Cup Player of the year at the Bulldogs 2015: Renouf was in the 17 for the Junior Kiwis and played as a back row/prop ===2018=== Atoni made his [[NRL]] debut in Round 10 2018 against the [[Parramatta Eels]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/round-10-nrl-teams-every-team-named/news-story/f9ddd4140aaaaa8cb1c623d04aaf4935 |title=Round 10 NRL teams:Every team named |work=Fox Sports |date=8 May 2018 |access-date=8 May 2018}} On 23 September 2018, Atoni was part of the Canterbury side which defeated Newtown 18-12 in the 2018 [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] grand final. The following week, To'omaga was part of the Canterbury side which defeated [[Queensland Cup]] winners Redcliffe 42-18 in the [[NRL State Championship]] final.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-intrust-super-championship/2018/state-championship-final/bulldogs-v-dolphins/|title=NRL Intrust Super Championship|website=www.nrl.com}}{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/bulldogs-cap-grand-final-win-over-jets-with-on-field-prop-osal-20180923-p505if.html/|title=Bulldogs cap grand final win over Jets with on-field prop-osal|website=www.smh.com.au|date=23 September 2018 }} In 2018 was named in the Intrust Team of the year at #10 prop. ===2019=== On 6 May 2019, Atoni was named in the [[Canterbury Cup NSW]] residents team to play against the Queensland residents side.{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/05/06/nswrl-names-canterbury-cup-nsw-residents-team/|title=NSWRL names Canterbury Cup NSW Residents team|website=www.nswrl.com.au|date=6 May 2019 }} ===2020=== In round 2 of the [[2020 NRL season]], he scored his first try in the [[NRL]] as Canterbury-Bankstown were defeated by [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] 24-16 in front of an empty [[ANZ Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/nrl/story/_/id/28925035/jason-taumalolo-man-cowboys-beat-bulldogs/|title=Jason Taumalolo man NQ beat Canterbury|website=www.espn.com|date=19 March 2020 }} He made a total of 16 appearances for Canterbury in the [[2020 NRL season]]. The club finished in 15th place on the table, only avoiding the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]] by for and against.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/nrl-2020-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-announce-eight-player-departures-kieran-foran-aiden-tolman-full-list/news-story/67fbadc55112e80ee342e26861bd206d?fbclid=IwAR0j-ofZNc_yFpjOkCEKeXn5uCy1T8rVDj664RO64BlUqgZQl5547RSu6ZM/|title=Dogs confirm EIGHT-man clean-out in first glimpse of Barrett era|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=22 September 2020 }} ===2021=== In July 2021, Atoni signed a contract to join the [[Sydney Roosters]] starting in 2022.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-bulldogs-confirm-departing-players-dylan-napa-lachlan-lewis-will-hopoate-roster-contracts/news-story/0f92571846a3b360fa3df2cd52db5dd8/|title=Bulldogs show Origin star the door in big clean-out; Sharks lock in winger: NRL Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} ===2022=== Atoni made no appearances for the Sydney Roosters during the [[2022 NRL season]]. Atoni instead featured for the clubs [[NSW Cup]] team [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] playing a total of 14 matches.{{cite web|url=https://www.sharks.com.au/news/2022/05/09/jets-swoop-late-to-overrun-bears/|title=Jets swoop late to overrun Bears|website=www.sharks.com.au|date=8 May 2022 }} On 30 November, Atoni signed a one-year deal with English side [[Wakefield Trinity]].{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/wakefield-trinity-confirm-signing-of-nrl-forward-63325/|title=Wakefield Trinity confirm signing of NRL forward|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=30 November 2022 }} ===2023=== Atoni made his club debut for Wakefield Trinity in their 24-38 loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in round 1 of the 2023 Super League season.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668377|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 24-38 Catalans Dragons - Tom Johnstone scores debut hat-trick against old club|work=BBC Sport |date=17 February 2023 }} Atoni played 21 matches for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}} ===2024=== In the 2024 RFL Championship season, Atoni would play 28 games in total as the club won the 1895 Cup, the league leaders shield and the RFL Championship grand final.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqjr5lvxk4lo|title=Jowitt breaks points record as Wakefield cruise to Grand Final win|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=19 October 2024 }} ===2025=== Atoni starred for [[Wakefield Trinity]] to secure a 15-14 win in round 20 against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], running for over 100 metres and making 25 tackles from the bench. Atoni played 17 games for Wakefield Trinity in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished sixth and qualified for the playoffs. Atoni played in the clubs elimination playoff loss to Leigh which was also his final game for the club. On 29 September 2025, it was reported that he had signed for Wakefield's arch-rivals [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on a two-year deal.{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=29 Sep 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Castleford Tigers sign Renouf Atoni from rivals Wakefield Trinity|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-sign-renouf-atoni-from-rivals-wakefield-trinity/}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.bulldogs.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/canterbury-bankstown-bulldogs/renouf-atoni/ Bulldogs profile] *[https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2021/01/20/from-toomaga-to-atoni-the-story-behind-renoufs-name-change/ From To'omaga to Atoni: The story behind Renouf's name change] {{Castleford Tigers squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Atoni, Renouf}} [[Category:1995 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century New Zealand sportsmen]] [[Category:Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs players]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:New Zealand people of Samoan descent]] [[Category:New Zealand people of Tokelauan descent]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Porirua]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Samoan descent]] [[Category:Wakefield Trinity players]]
